message:
fix(agents,analysis): add read-error reporting to agents_check and remove redundant into_iter

src/agents.rs:
- Extract core logic from `agents_check` into a new private helper
  `agents_check_from_detection(work_dir, &AgentFileDetection)` so the
  detection result can be injected directly, enabling deterministic
  unit-testing without touching the filesystem.
- Add an early-return branch inside `agents_check_from_detection` that
  handles `det.read_error == true`: when an agent file is found but
  cannot be read (e.g. permission denied), the function now returns a
  clear message ("Found {file_type} at {path}, but it could not be
  read. Check file permissions before running any agents command.")
  with `changed: false`, instead of falling through to logic that
  assumes the file contents are available.
- The public `agents_check` function is preserved as a thin wrapper
  that calls `detect_agent_file_in_parents` then delegates to the new
  helper, so the external API is unchanged.
- Add test `agents_check_reports_unreadable_agent_file` that constructs
  an `AgentFileDetection` with `read_error: true` and asserts the
  returned message contains "could not be read" and "Check file
  permissions", and that `changed` is false.

src/analysis/graph.rs:
- Remove redundant `.into_iter()` on `self.dependents(&id)` in the
  `.chain()` call inside `IssueGraph`; `.chain()` accepts any
  `IntoIterator`, so the explicit conversion is unnecessary (clippy
  `clippy::useless_conversion` lint).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
